idea.topic: Light Travel Time & Perception of Time
facts: Light from our Sun takes ~8 minutes 20 seconds to reach Earth. You're seeing it *as it was* 8m 20s ago.
The brightest star, Sirius, is ~8.6 light-years away. When you look at it, you're seeing light that left it *8.6 years ago*.
The Andromeda Galaxy is ~2.5 million light-years away. Its image shows it *2.5 million years in the past*.
idea.payoff: Every photon reaching your eye is a messenger from the past. The farther you look, the deeper into history you see. The 'present' is a tiny, ever-shifting bubble around you.
idea.title: Your 'Now' Is a Lie! 🤯⏱️

scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with subtle, slow-moving distant stars. A single bright star twinkles.

scenes.text: Sun's light: ~8m 20s to Earth. You see it *8m 20s in the past*.
scenes.visual: Animated graphic: Sun on left, Earth on right. A light beam travels from Sun to Earth with an animated timer (0:00 -> 8:20).

scenes.text: Sirius: ~8.6 light-years away. You see light that left it *8.6 years ago*.
scenes.visual: Image of the bright star Sirius against a deep space backdrop. A light beam extends from Sirius towards the viewer, labeled '8.6 years'.

scenes.text: Andromeda Galaxy: ~2.5 million light-years away. You see it *2.5 million years in the past*.
scenes.visual: Stunning, high-resolution image of the Andromeda Galaxy. Text '2.5 MILLION YEARS AGO' subtly pulses near the galaxy image.

scenes.text: Every photon = past messenger. Look farther = deeper into history. Your 'present' is a tiny, shifting bubble.
scenes.visual: A stylized human eye looking out into an expanding cosmic view. Light rays converge towards the eye, showing galaxies and nebulae fading into the distance.
